metadata.title: States PCS: Bihar GK for BPSC and other Bihar PCS Exams

questions.question: Which Veda mentioned about the Anga Kingdom for the first time?
questions.explanation: Atharvaveda mentioned about the Anga Kingdom for the first time.

questions.question: Which is the most important sutra for the Pure Land Schools of Buddhism?
questions.explanation: Sukhavati-vyuha is the most important sutra for the Pure Land Schools of Buddhism. It is also called as Pure Land Sutra. In this sutra, The Buddha tells Ananda about Amitabha and his Pure Land or heaven and how one can be reborn there.

questions.question: In which of the following city, Mahavira held his second Samavasarana?
questions.explanation: Mahavira held his second Samavasarana in the garden of Mahasena in Pava city. Samavasarana means a preaching pavillion to spread knowledge among the common people. At Pava city, his words of wisdom resonated with the masses and eleven Brahmins chose to embrace his preaching and convert to Jainism. Lord Mahavira imparted the Tripadi Knowledge (Three Pronouncements) to his chief disciples which were Upaneiva (Emergence), Vigameiva (Distruction) and Dhuveiva (Permanence).

questions.question: Which of the following was a large Agro-industrial town in Undivided Bihar?
questions.explanation: Dalmianagar was a large Agro-industrial town in Undivided Bihar. It is in Rohtas District of Bihar. The Industrial town of Dalmianagar was founded by the industrialist Ramkrishna Dalmia, a doyens of business in 20th century India and founder of the Dalmia group. Dalmianagar developed into a massive industrial town from the 1940s till the 1980s with factories producing sugar, cement, paper, chemicals, vanaspati etc. and employing top professionals of the country. Dalmianagar had housing colony, gardens, clubs, schools, market complexes, and hospitals for its employees. The prosperity of Dalmianagar started to decline in the mid 1970s as the law and order situation in this industrial town began to worsen. By the mid 1980s factories started to shut down and By the 1990s Dalmianagar looked like a ghost town.

questions.question: In which town/city, First Oil refinery of Bihar was established?
questions.explanation: The First Oil refinery of Bihar was established in Barauni. Barauni is loacted in Begusarai district of Bihar. Barauni Refinery was the dream project of the Architect of Modern Bihar Dr. Shrikrishna Sinha ,the 1st chief minister of Bihar.

questions.question: Under Which Schedule of the Constitution of India, Maithili has been recognized as one of the regional languages of India?
questions.explanation: Maithili has been recognized as one of the regional languages of India under the 8th Schedule of the Constitution of India. Maithili is an Indo-Aryan language mainly spoken in the states of Bihar and Jharkhand. The Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India lists the official languages of the Republic of India. As per Articles 344(1) and 351 of the Indian constitution, the eighth schedule includes the recognition of the 22 languages including Maithili.

questions.question: Which was the first Urdu newspaper of Bihar?
questions.explanation: The first Urdu newspaper of Bihar was Nurul Anwar. It was published by Mohammad Hashim from Ara.

questions.question: Who was called “Dadamuni”?
questions.explanation: Ashok Kumar was called “Dadamoni”. He was born on 13 October 1911. He was born to a Bengali family in Bhagalpur, then in the Bengal Presidency of British India and now in the Bihar. He was an Indian film actor who attained iconic status in Indian cinema. He was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1988. He also received the Padma Bhushan in 1999 for his contributions to Indian cinema. He is considered to be one of India’s finest actors ever, playing leading, negative and character roles with equal panache.

questions.question: In which year, Nava Nalanda Mahavihara was established?
questions.explanation: Nava Nalanda Mahavihar was established in 1951. It is an institute deemed to be university located in Nalanda, Bihar. It was built to revive the ancient seat of learning in Nalanda. Nava Nalanda Mahavihar was founded to develop as a centre of higher studies in Pali and Buddhism. Nava Nalanda Mahavihar renowned for Pali Research Institute.

questions.question: In which city/town, Brahmyoni hill is situated?
questions.explanation: Brahmyoni hill is situated in Gaya. It is considered as a sacred hill. Mahabharata and Buddhist records have mentioned about this hill. At Brahmyoni hill, Buddha preached the Fire Sermon (Adittapariyaya Sutta) to a thousand former fire-worshipping ascetics. They all became enlightened while listening to this discourse.
